content: @Alice Ryhl , I'm looking at workqueue code, do you have a usage of the HasWork::get_work_offset function? In other words, is there any Unsize type impl HasWork?
No, and I don't actually think that !Sized workqueue items work right now.
If the struct work_struct locates in "sized part" of a !Sized, I think it might still work (the same as the Sized case). But for a more general !Sized it doesn't work, right?
Since the function pointer in the Work knows the concrete type, !Sized can definitely work.
Oh, yes, it can work.
but currently work_container_of requires Self: Sized.
But work_container_of is only used in cases where we know the concrete type.
(inside the function pointer I mentioned)
Right. Two things I want to bring up while I'm looking at it: 1) work_container_of can work on some !Sized type as well. and 2) raw_get_work seems better use get_work_offset instead of OFFSET.
raw_get_work requires Sized, so how it gets the offset doesn't matter
There's no way to make it object safe since it doesn't take a self parameter.

Alice Ryhl said:

raw_get_work requires Sized, so how it gets the offset doesn't matter

Hmm.. does raw_get_work requires Sized, I must miss the requirement somewhere?
If it's not listed, then the trait is not object safe.

Boqun Feng said:

Alice Ryhl said:

raw_get_work requires Sized, so how it gets the offset doesn't matter

Hmm.. does raw_get_work requires Sized, I must miss the requirement somewhere?

Oh, right, you cast *mut Self to a thin pointer.

Alice Ryhl said:

If it's not listed, then the trait is not object safe.

I looked into "object safety", looks like HasWork is not technically object safe anyway, since it has a associated constant.
You may want to compare to my linked list. That one supports dyn Trait in linked list entries.
Well, looks like your linked list doesn't have a get_xxx_offset function in the HasXX trait, so I think we can remove the one for HasWork as well?
sure

Hmm.. but if we were to follow that object safety doc, I think we should instead provide a get_xxx_offset function and remove OFFSET constant.

cc @Gary Guo @bjorn3
You can't have OFFSET with dyn Trait because different concrete structs may have different offsets.

Alice Ryhl said:

You can't have OFFSET with dyn Trait because different concrete structs may have different offsets.

Yeah, that's why I figured that "object safety" requires no associated contants.
Note that in Binder, I force them to have the same constant by using Arc<Wrapper<dyn Trait>> with Wrapper<T> having a T field at the end.
Having to go through a dynamic method call for every offset is really expensive, it turns out.
Ah, this is the typical case where I'd want a method with default body for Sized type only
I think we probably should remove get_work_offset
Maybe also remove OFFSET, and just move the default impl logic into macros?
